---
title: "Audiological Data — Quality Report"
date: today
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    max-width: 1600px
    self-contained: true
    output-file: data_quality_report.html
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false

---

```{r setup}
library(arsenal)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Load data
# The Age column is read as character because it contains a mix of integer
# ages and 4-digit birth years (data entry errors).
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
data_path <- file.path("..", "data", "aud_data_simulated.csv")
if (!file.exists(data_path)) {
  data_path <- file.path("data", "aud_data_simulated.csv")
}

d <- read.csv(data_path, colClasses = c(Age = "character"))

# Treat Group and Sex as factors with informative labels
d$Group <- factor(d$Group, levels = 1:3,
                  labels = c("No tinnitus", "Mild tinnitus", "Severe tinnitus"))
d$Sex   <- factor(d$Sex, levels = 1:3,
                  labels = c("Male", "Female", "Other"))

# Hearing threshold column names (left and right ears)
l_cols <- paste0("L", c(125, 250, 500, 1000, 2000, 3000, 4000, 6000, 8000))
r_cols <- paste0("R", c(125, 250, 500, 1000, 2000, 3000, 4000, 6000, 8000))
```


## Descriptive table by tinnitus group

Data stratified by tinnitus severity group in table to highlight any data quality issues.

```{r arsenal-table, results = "asis"}
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Prepare analysis dataset:
#   - Convert Age to numeric; participants with birth-year entries become NA
#   - Exclude the ID column from the summary
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
d_analysis        <- d
d_analysis$Age    <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(d$Age))

# Build formula: Group ~ Age + Sex + all hearing threshold columns
rhs    <- paste(c("Age", "Sex", l_cols, r_cols), collapse = " + ")
f      <- as.formula(paste("Group ~", rhs))

# Arsenal tableby — continuous vars: median (Q1, Q3); cat vars: count (%)
tab <- tableby(
  formula  = f,
  data     = d_analysis,
  numeric.stats = c("medianrange", "Nmiss"),
  cat.stats     = "countpct",
  test          = FALSE
)

summary(tab, digits = 1)
```


## Data quality issues

Two types of data quality issues can be identified in the table above:

1. Birth year recorded instead of age (age maximum is > 150 years for two participants).
2. Missing right-ear data for one subject.

```{r birthyear-table}
# Identify rows where Age cannot be coerced to a plausible age in years.
# A 4-digit birth year (>= 1900) is the expected error pattern.
age_numeric <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(d$Age))
birthyear_ids <- d$ID[!is.na(age_numeric) & age_numeric >= 1900]

kable(
  data.frame(
    ID          = birthyear_ids,
    Age_as_entered = d$Age[d$ID %in% birthyear_ids]
  ),
  col.names = c("Subject ID", "Value entered in Age column"),
  caption   = "Participants with birth year recorded instead of age"
)
```

```{r missing-ear-table}
# Identify participants where every right-ear threshold is NA
all_r_missing <- apply(d[, r_cols], 1, function(x) all(is.na(x)))
missing_ids   <- d$ID[all_r_missing]

# For each affected participant, detect which columns are actually NA
missing_col_strings <- sapply(which(all_r_missing), function(row) {
  paste(names(d)[is.na(d[row, ])], collapse = ", ")
})

kable(
  data.frame(
    ID              = missing_ids,
    Missing_columns = missing_col_strings
  ),
  col.names = c("Subject ID", "Missing columns"),
  caption   = "Participants with complete right-ear data missing"
)
```

---
